 {"id":"bd-jrgbe","title":"Add golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and parse errors","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:35:39.800324358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.722101004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.719925425Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and ParseError messages; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-types representative_scalar_and_parse_error_exact_golden_contract -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jtt2","title":"Implement external journal paired replay (not just UUID validation)","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 filesystems can use an external journal device (JOURNAL_DEV incompat flag on the data FS, journal_dev field points to device number, journal_uuid links the pair). When crash recovery is needed, the JBD2 journal blocks live on the external device rather than in an internal inode.

CURRENT STATE: OpenOptions::external_journal_path accepts an external journal image path. maybe_probe_external_journal opens it, reads its JBD2 superblock, validates UUID pairing and block size match, and stores ExternalJournalInfo on OpenFs. But maybe_replay_ext4_journal short-circuits when journal_dev != 0 — it logs 'journal replay skipped' and returns Ok(None). No actual replay occurs.

The log message honestly states: 'external journal UUID validation only; full paired replay not yet implemented'.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is Some AND UUIDs match:
   a. Open the external journal image as a ByteDevice.
   b. Parse the JBD2 superblock from the external device.
   c. Compute journal segments: the entire external device (minus superblock) is the journal area.
   d. Feed the segments to replay_jbd2_segments the same way we do for internal journals.
   e. The replay writes go to the DATA filesystem's device, not the journal device.
2. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery IS needed:
   - Currently returns UnsupportedFeature error (good — fail-fast).
   - No change needed here.
3. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery NOT needed:
   - Currently returns Ok(None) with info log (good — graceful skip).
   - No change needed here.

KEY CONSIDERATIONS:
- The external journal's block layout is different from an internal journal. The JBD2 superblock is at block 0 of the device, and journal blocks start immediately after. There's no inode or extent mapping.
- We need to compute JournalSegment { start, blocks } for the external device based on the JBD2 superblock's s_maxlen field.
- The replay must write to the data device, not the journal device. The existing replay infrastructure takes a BlockDevice for writes — we just need to pass the data device.
- After successful replay, we should clear the needs_recovery flag on the data device's superblock.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic data FS + external journal pair, dirties the journal, and verifies replay applies blocks to the data device.
- The log message changes from 'UUID validation only' to 'external journal replay completed'.
- UUID mismatch still fails fast.
- Block size mismatch still fails fast.
- ExternalJournalInfo is populated with journal metadata after successful replay.
- The 'required crash recovery' error path still works when no external path is provided.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:40.642853327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763994355Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763527321Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete. External journal paired replay is fully implemented: UUID validation, segment computation (bd-vdk6), and replay wiring (bd-vql1) are all done. 4 tests covering success path, UUID mismatch, dirty FS requirement, and clean FS skip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vdk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:55.294301302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vql1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:56.821268230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-juy7","title":"Add ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial corpus coverage for ext4 inode checksum stamping/verification, corruption rejection, and size-boundary failures in crates/ffs-ondisk/tests/adversarial_corpus.rs; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:36:09.623765671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.215554859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.213412544Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuzz","ondisk","testing"]}
-{"id":"bd-jzxq1","title":"[HIGH] fuzz_ext4_checksums fails to compile - API drift","description":"Fuzz target fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ext4_checksums.rs:26 calls verify_group_desc_checksum with wrong signature. Function at crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs:1735 signature changed but fuzz target not updated. Current call: verify_group_desc_checksum(data, sb.checksum_seed, 0, 64). Missing arguments: uuid (&[u8; 16]), desc_size (u16), checksum_kind (Ext4GroupDescChecksumKind).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:19.859993342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:34:27.999101873Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:34:27.999101873Z","close_reason":"Updated fuzz_ext4_checksums to the current verify_group_desc_checksum API using the parsed ext4 superblock uuid, descriptor size, and checksum kind; verified with rch cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_ext4_checksums and rch cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_ext4_checksums -- -D warnings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-k1r7x","title":"revalidate stale xfstests ioctl allowlist entries after FIEMAP and SETFLAGS coverage landed","description":"Survey finding: `scripts/e2e/xfstests_allowlist.json` still says `ext4/001` is blocked because FUSE does not forward `FIEMAP`, and `ext4/005` is blocked because inode flag manipulation is unsupported, but `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now prove mounted-path `FIEMAP` and `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` / `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` coverage.

Scope:
- Re-run the relevant xfstests or close analogues against the current FUSE path.
- Replace stale allowlist reasons with the actual remaining blocker, if any, instead of the old pre-coverage assumption.
- Update docs/notes so xfstests triage matches current FrankenFS ioctl behavior.
- Keep explicit separation between transport-layer kernel limits and missing FrankenFS behavior.

Acceptance:
- `ext4/001` and `ext4/005` have current, evidence-backed allowlist notes or are removed from the allowlist.
- No xfstests note claims missing FIEMAP or inode-flag support when the repo already proves that surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:34.125296714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:36.236299402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:36.234126017Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fuse","ioctl","xfstests"]}

 {"id":"bd-k2xhy","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for fallocate mutation attempts","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e covers writable fallocate success and invalid/unsupported mode rejection, but not the mounted-path read-only contract even though ffs-fuse rejects fallocate with EROFS before dispatch. Scope: add ext4 read-only probes that attempt representative fallocate mutations such as preallocate and keep-size or punch-hole forms, asserting exact EROFS with unchanged apparent size and file contents. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only refusal for fallocate mutation attempts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:33:02.558777625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:01:08.231928562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:01:08.230039713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
